entry.question: What specific locality in Spain appears as Francisco Naveda Somarriba's place of origin in contemporaneous records from Papantla, Veracruz, Mexico, or in Spanish emigration records?
entry.rationale: The research objective requires identifying where in Spain Francisco was born or baptized. The specific locality is a gatekeeper fact: without it, Spanish parish records cannot be searched for his baptism or his parents. Papantla church records (including the already-sourced 1862 baptism of his daughter) commonly recorded a Spanish-born father's place of origin, and the BYU Immigrant Ancestors Project entry may supply or corroborate this detail. Spanish emigration records from the mid-19th century and Mexican civil or ecclesiastical records are the primary sources. This question must be answered before Q2 (parentage via Spanish parish records) can be meaningfully pursued.

entry.question: Who were the parents of Francisco Naveda Somarriba, as named in his baptism record from his Spanish locality of origin or in other contemporaneous original records?
entry.rationale: Identifying Francisco's parents is the second half of the research objective. The primary source is his baptism record from his Spanish hometown (to be located by Q1); secondary sources include the 1862 Papantla baptism of his daughter (which may record the child's paternal grandparents), other Papantla church records, and the BYU Immigrant Ancestors Project entry. This question depends on Q1 because the Spanish parish search strategy requires knowing the locality.

entry.rationale: The BYU Immigrant Ancestors Project entry for Francisco Naveda y [Somarriba] (src-2, http://immigrants.byu.edu/search/view_newrecord/836925) is already in the project sources but has not been extracted. BYU's project systematically compiled Spanish emigrants to Latin America, often recording the specific town of origin in Spain. This entry is the highest-probability quick answer — review and extract it first before searching new sources.

The 1862 baptism of daughter Maria Valentina (src-1, collection 1883382) is sourced but not yet extracted. Mexican Catholic baptism registers routinely named the father's place of origin in Spain, and the child's paternal grandparents (Francisco's parents) were often recorded as well. Extracting this record may simultaneously answer q_001 and q_002. Additionally, searching the same Papantla parish collection for Francisco's other children's baptisms, his marriage to Maria Josefa Silvera, or any burial record will multiply the chances of finding his stated origin.

Mexico, Veracruz, Civil Registration, 1821-1963 (collection 1922413) covers the period after Mexican civil registration began (1857 Reform Laws). Civil birth, marriage, and death records sometimes recorded a foreign-born party's place of origin more explicitly than church records. Search for Francisco Naveda or Naveda Somarriba in Papantla/Veracruz civil records. A marriage record with Maria Josefa Silvera or a death record would be especially valuable.

PARES Movimientos Migratorios Iberoamericanos (http://pares.mcu.es/MovimientosMigratorios/) is the Spanish government's online database of emigration records drawn from passenger lists, notarial acts, and consular records. It covers departures from Spanish ports to Latin America and often records the emigrant's town of origin, age, and family. Search for Francisco Naveda and variant spellings. This is a free, indexed resource covering the period when Francisco emigrated (~1856).

Full-text co-occurrence search for the surnames Naveda AND Somarriba across the entire FamilySearch Spanish-records corpus, executed via the search-full-text skill. Francisco's compound surname 'Naveda Somarriba' encodes his father's surname (Naveda) and his mother's surname (Somarriba). A co-occurrence query (+Naveda +Somarriba) will surface his own baptism, his parents' marriage, and his siblings' baptisms — regardless of whether the index captures his name — because both surnames appear together in any act from this family. Both surnames are Cantabrian toponyms (Naveda is a village in Cantabria; Somarriba is a valley/district there), making this the highest-yield item once indexed name searches fail. Do NOT scope to a specific collection or phrase; search unscoped across the full corpus.

Spain, Cantabria, Passports, 1785-1863 (collection 2000617, 24,806 images, browse-only) records passport applications from the Province of Santander/Cantabria — the most likely region of origin given that 'Naveda' and 'Somarriba' are both Cantabrian place-names. Francisco emigrated around 1856, squarely within this date range. Passport applications named the applicant's municipality of origin, age, and purpose of travel. Browse by estimated emigration years 1854-1858.

Spain, Consular Records of Emigrants, 1808-1960 (collection 1928310, 105,083 images, browse-only) is a national-level series of consular emigration records that may document Francisco's departure from Spain with his specific town of origin. Fallback for the Cantabria Passports (pli_006) if that browse search proves unmanageable or he is not found there.

Spain, Diocese of Santander, Catholic Church Records, 1538-1985 (collection 2078544, 2.7M persons, 706K images) covers all of historic Cantabria. If earlier steps confirm a Cantabrian origin but do not identify the specific parish, search this collection for baptisms of a Francisco Naveda (father's surname) born around 1830 whose mother bore the surname Somarriba. This collection is the primary vehicle for confirming the locality hypothesis and locating his actual baptism record once the region is confirmed.

fields.informant: one or both parents of Maria Valentina Naveda, presenting the child for baptism
fields.informant_bias_notes: Parent(s) presenting a child for baptism chose and know the name being conferred; this is first-hand knowledge regardless of which parent spoke to the priest.
Parish priest officiated and recorded the baptism he personally performed; his proximity is witness, not official_duty (the lookup table for church registers specifies 'witness' for the clergyman).

fields.informant: Dn. Francisco Naveda, presenting himself as the child's father at the baptism
fields.informant_bias_notes: The father-child relationship is stated by the father himself at the moment of presenting the child; the priest records what he was told. Francisco is the informant for his own parentage role, not the priest.

fields.informant: unknown person who registered Eugenio's death at the civil registry
fields.informant_bias_notes: The civil index does not identify the registrant. Could be Francisco or Josefa (parents) who had direct knowledge, or a neighbor. Informant identity unknown.
Registrant unknown. Birth year is derived by subtraction from the child's stated age at death, not directly stated — compounding the indeterminacy of the informant with the arithmetic inference.

fields.informant: unknown person who registered Eugenio's death at the civil registry
fields.informant_bias_notes: Registrant unknown. A parent registering a child's death would have been a witness (primary). A neighbor or third party would be secondary. Cannot determine which applies.

Registrant identity unknown from the FamilySearch index. If it was Francisco himself registering his son's death, his own name would be primary (self-report). If a sibling or neighbor, secondary. Indeterminate until the image is read.

Same unknown registrant. Josefa Silvera's name as mother of the deceased is plausible but indeterminate until the image identifies the registrant.

Maria Naveda (bride) names her father Francisco Naveda. She grew up in his household and has direct personal knowledge of his name. 'self' was incorrect — 'self' means the informant IS the subject; here the subject is Francisco, not Maria. 'household_member' correctly captures her relationship.

fields.informant: Maria Naveda (bride), naming her father at civil marriage registration

A person cannot provide primary information about their own birth (not cognitively aware during the event). Maria stating her own birth year in 1895 is secondary information regardless of how confidently she reported it.
Same rule as a_016: Donato cannot have primary knowledge of his own birth. Birth year reported on his own marriage registration is secondary.

Donato Márquez names his father Esteban Márquez. Subject of this assertion is Esteban, not Donato. 'self' was incorrect; 'household_member' captures Donato's direct personal knowledge of his father's name from growing up in his household.

entry.rationale: a_001 names the baptized child as 'Maria Valentina Josefa Teofila de Jesus Naveda'. p-child-1 is Maria Valentina Josefa Teofila de Jesus Naveda Silvera, baptized 18 Jan 1862 in Papantla, Veracruz — the same event. Name, date, place, and event type all match exactly. Strong match.
a_002 records the baptism event (18 Jan 1862, Papantla, Veracruz, Mexico) of the same child named in a_001. The date and place match p-child-1's known baptism exactly.

a_003 names 'Dn. Francisco Naveda' as father of the baptized child at Papantla, 18 Jan 1862. PID-TODO is Francisco Naveda Somarriba, known to reside in Papantla, Veracruz c.1862 with wife Maria Josefa Silvera. Name matches (surname 'Naveda'); place and date match known residence facts exactly; wife Josefa Silvera appears in a_005 from the same record. Strong match.

a_004 asserts Francisco Naveda is father of Maria Valentina at the 1862 Papantla baptism. Consistent with PID-TODO (Francisco Naveda Somarriba), known Papantla resident c.1862. The relationship role 'father_of_baptized' corroborates his identity as the father.

a_004 also bears on the child side of the father-daughter relationship: Maria Valentina Naveda (p-child-1) is the child named in this relationship assertion. Same record, same event, same identifiers.

a_005 names 'Doña Maria Josefa Silvoro Morales' as mother of the baptized child at Papantla, 1862. p-spouse is Maria Josefa Silvera. 'Silvoro' is a phonetic/spelling variant of 'Silvera' — a common transcription variant in 19th-century Mexican records. Given name 'Maria Josefa' matches exactly, and she is co-present with Francisco (PID-TODO) as expected from the known couple. Strong match.

a_006 names 'Eugenio Naveda' as the deceased in the 6 Jul 1875 Papantla civil death registration, son of Francisco Naveda and Josefa Silvera. I1 is a new stub created for this person. No independent corroboration yet; one source only. Parents match PID-TODO and p-spouse exactly. Probable confidence for a new stub with single-record evidence.
a_007 records Eugenio Naveda's birth (estimated ~1873, from age at death). Same reasoning as a_006 — single-record stub (I1). Birth year inferred from age, so indirect evidence.
a_008 records Eugenio Naveda's death (6 Jul 1875, Papantla, Veracruz, Mexico). Same as a_006 — single-record stub (I1). Death date and place directly stated.

a_009 names 'Francisco Naveda' as father of Eugenio Naveda in the 1875 Papantla civil death registration. PID-TODO (Francisco Naveda Somarriba) is the only known Francisco Naveda in Papantla with wife Josefa Silvera (named in a_011 from the same record). Consistent with the 1862 baptism (src_001) where Francisco and Josefa appear as parents. Name, place, wife's name, and role all agree. Strong match.

a_010 asserts Francisco Naveda is father of the deceased Eugenio Naveda. Same reasoning as a_009: PID-TODO is the only Francisco Naveda in Papantla documented with wife Josefa Silvera. Relationship role as father corroborates the identification.
a_010 also bears on the child side: Eugenio Naveda (I1) is the child in this father-son relationship assertion. Same one-source stub as a_006–a_008.

a_011 names 'Josefa Silvera' as mother of Eugenio Naveda in the 1875 Papantla civil death registration. p-spouse is Maria Josefa Silvera. The surname 'Silvera' matches exactly; given name abbreviated to 'Josefa'. Consistent with her role as mother in the 1862 baptism (a_005). Name, place, and family context all agree. Strong match.

a_012 names 'Francisco Naveda' as father of bride Maria Naveda in the 15 Nov 1895 Papantla civil marriage registration. PID-TODO (Francisco Naveda Somarriba) matches: name agrees, wife Josefa Silvera appears in a_014 from the same record, and Francisco is documented as Maria's father across three independent records (1862 baptism, 1875 death, 1895 marriage). a_013 also bears on the child side: Maria Naveda (I2) is the bride/child in this father-daughter relationship assertion. New stub from a single record.

args.notes: 36 total hits. Key finds: (1) Baptism of Balentina Josefa, 17 Dec 1833, Limpias, Cantabria — daughter of Juan Navedas Somarriba + María Somarriba González; paternal grandparents Luís de Naveda + María Cruz Somarriba of Carasa, Voto, Santander (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W); (2) 1843 re-marriage of Juan de Naveda at Limpias as widower, confirming same parentage (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX). These place the Naveda Somarriba family squarely in Limpias/Carasa, Junta de Voto, Province of Santander, Cantabria.

query.scope: unscoped — full FamilySearch FTS corpus, continuation of log_005

args.notes: Critical hit: Death of Doña María de Somarriba González, 11 Oct 1834, Limpias, Cantabria — wife of Don Juan de Naveda; in their marriage they had 'Francisco y Valentina' (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D137-1PP). Francisco Naveda Somarriba is the son of Juan de Naveda and María de Somarriba González. Also found: 'Francisco Naveda Somarriba' named as godfather in Papantla, Mexico, 2 Apr 1877 (ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GG89-HSK) — confirms compound surname in Mexican records. Also: 1824 baptism in Hoz de Marrón names Luís de Naveda + María Cruz of Carasas as grandparents (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-DRR9-XZC), establishing the Naveda family in Carasa/Voto.

Extract from four FTS records found in pli_005 search. Project: /private/var/folders/g7/zn65fvrn5g9_g71_sp0lk82h0000gn/T/e2e-naveda-spain-origin-c7o9z2pg

RECORD 1 — HIGHEST PRIORITY (answers q_001 and q_002 directly):
ARK: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D137-1PP
Collection: Spain, Santander, Deaths, from 1 February 1500 to 2018 (collectionId: M9J1-WJ7)
Title: Limpias, Santander, Spain — Church Death, 1834
Log entry: log_006 (search that found it)
verbatim textDocument: "En el Campo Santo de la Iglesia Parroquial de esta Villa de Limpias a once de Octre de mil ochocientos treinta y cuatro se enterró Doña María de Somarriba González que murió hoy mismo del Colera morbo de edad de treinta y dos años : recibió solamente el Sacramento de Penitencia . No hizo testamento que legítima consorte de Don Juan de Naveda, en cuyo matrimonio tuvieron a Francisco y a Valentina . Se le hizo entierro mayor con Novenario : y por que conste lo firmo fecha ut supra Entre renglones de esta vecindad = Don Joseph del Rivero"

RECORD 2 — HIGH PRIORITY (corroborates parentage; answers q_002):
ARK: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W
Collection: Spain, Catholic Church Records, 1307-2005 (collectionId: 1784529)
Title: San Pedro, Limpias, Cantabria, Spain — Baptism, 17 December 1833
Log entry: log_005
verbatim textDocument (key passage): "En la Villa de Limpias a diez y siete de Diciembre de mil ochocientos treinta y tres Yo D. José del Rivera Collado, Cura beneficiado … Bauticé … a una niña que nació ayer, a quien puse por nombre y Balentina Josefa, hija legítima de D. Juan Navedas Somarriba y Doña María Somarriba González, su mujer, de esta vecindad : nieta paterna de D. Luís de Naveda y Doña María Cruz Somarriba : materna de Don Francisco Somarriba, y Doña Josefa González Aguirre, todos vecinos de Carasa, Voto, Santander, España : fueron padrinos Don José Somarriba, del mismo Carasa, y Doña Mariana de la Piedra"

RECORD 3 — HIGH PRIORITY (confirms Juan de Naveda's parentage as brother/father-figure):
ARK: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX
Collection: Spain, Catholic Church Records, 1307-2005 (collectionId: 1784529)
Title: San Pedro, Limpias, Cantabria, Spain — Church Marriage, 7 August 1843
Log entry: log_005
verbatim textDocument (key passage): "Don Juan de Naveda, viudo de Doña María de Somarriba González, hijo legítimo de D. Luís de Naveda y Doña María Cruz de Somarriba, vecinos que … fueron el lugar de Carasa, Junta de Voto"

RECORD 4 — CONFIRMATORY (Francisco Naveda Somarriba in Papantla, Mexico, 1877):
ARK: ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GG89-HSK
Collection: Mexico, Veracruz, Catholic Church Records, 1590-1978 (collectionId: 1883382)
Title: Papantla, Veracruz, Mexico — Baptism, 2 April 1877
Log entry: log_006
verbatim textDocument (key passage): "bauticé solemnemente … a María Francisca, Edelmira, Alidia, de un día de nacida, hija legítima de José Esteban Valdez y Caranco y de Carmen Domínguez y Gutiérrez, vecinos de esta Cabecera . Fueron sus padrinos Francisco Naveda Somarriba y Cándida García"

Research context: All records extracted for q_001 (Spanish locality of Francisco Naveda Somarriba) and q_002 (his parents). Francisco's parents are Juan de Naveda and María de Somarriba González; locality is Limpias, Province of Santander, Cantabria. Running --autonomous.
